name: Enriched marine oil raised human SPMs and reprogrammed leukocyte responses
summary: A randomized double-blind crossover study in healthy volunteers found dose- and time-dependent increases in peripheral blood SPMs and immune-cell response changes after enriched marine oil supplementation.
statement: In healthy volunteers, enriched marine oil supplementation produced time- and dose-dependent increases in peripheral blood SPM concentrations, increased neutrophil and monocyte bacterial phagocytosis, lowered leukocyte and platelet activation markers, and changed peripheral blood immune and metabolic transcript levels compared with placebo.
population_or_model: Healthy adult volunteers in a double-blind placebo-controlled crossover study
quantitative_note: The registry results posted in 2025 report mean peripheral blood SPM levels of 56.49 pg/mL after placebo and 139.7 pg/mL after the highest dose at 24 hours.
caveats: Participants were young healthy adults, not older adults with tissue-repair deficits.
The endpoints are target-engagement and immune-response biomarkers.
No tissue-repair, frailty, disease, or mortality outcome was tested.
